observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Create a new Inventory Committee who will conduct a complete physical
count of all PPE in accordance with the procedures stated in the “One Time
Cleansing of PPE” under COA Circular No. 2020-006;

b. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE, reconcile the same
with the records of the General Services Officer (GSO) and Accounting
Office and promptly submit a copy to COA for verification; and

c. Require the Municipal Accountant and MT to maintain the PPE Subsidiary
Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and timely
submission of all required monthly BRS. Furthermore, the Municipal
Accountant must maintain accurate SL for all bank accounts, thoroughly
review all records related to the Cash in Bank account and make any
necessary adjustments to correct the amounts reported in the FS and other
relevant documents; and

b. Require the MT to coordinate with the source agencies to verify the status
and remaining balances of non-moving accounts of the Municipality.
Thereafter, revert any remaining or unused balance in accordance with the
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Guidelines of the said fund to the
national treasury or unappropriated surplus of the GF of the Municipality,
as the case maybe.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately adjust the Agency’s
books by recognizing the completed Infrastructure Assets in the FS. A
journal entry must be made by debiting the appropriate Asset accounts and
crediting the CIP accounts (Infrastructure Assets/Building and Other
Structures) in the total amount of ₱14,970,000.00. Further, provide the
appropriate depreciation expenses for depreciable PPE accounts upon
effecting the required reclassification/adjustment;

b. Strengthen internal controls over the recording and classification of
infrastructure assets. This may involve establishing clear procedures for
asset verification, project completion documentation, timely and proper
recording of transactions, including the SLs, pursuant to the Revised Chart
of Accounts for LGUs; and

c. Direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare and submit the Quarterly
Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation
of LGSF-SBDP using the prescribed format as well as the separate SLs for
each beneficiary barangay and projects implemented as required under
DBM LBC No. 154 dated February 6, 2024.

We reiterated our recommendations that the LCE:

a. Direct the MT, LO and all Collecting Officers to immediately deposit/remit
all undeposited collections to the AGDB. Further, institute appropriate legal
actions as well as exhaust all available administrative remedies to ensure
prompt settlement of outstanding balances;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant and MT to reconcile any discrepancies
in the Cash in Local Treasury account and promptly make the necessary
correcting entries in their respective books. Furthermore, internal controls
over collections and remittances must be strengthened to prevent future
accumulation of unremitted collections; and

c. Require all AOs to strictly adhere with the aforecited rules and regulations
regarding handling of collections and remittance.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct all officers and employees with unliquidated CAs to immediately
settle their accounts or submit the required liquidation documents.
Otherwise, failure to do so may constitute a valid cause for the withholding
of their salary;

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor outstanding CAs
liquidation and regularly issue demand letters to all AOs and employees for
immediate liquidation/settlement of their outstanding cash advances; and

c. Conform with the procedures and requirements set forth in COA Circular
No. 2023-008 and immediately facilitate the request for write-off of dormant
CAs.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the MT and Municipal Accountant to maintain SCs and SLCs per
stock number, respectively, and regularly reconcile the balances of
inventory based on their respective records; and

b. Improve inventory management by establishing a functional Inventory
Committee and require the latter to adequately prepare the RPCI.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to make
the necessary adjusting entry to correct the noted misstatement by debiting Due to
NGAs account and crediting the Trust Liability account. Also, ensure that the
Municipality is compliant with the accounting guidelines set out for the grants
received from the national government agencies in subsequent transactions.

We recommended that Management ensure in the succeeding years that actual
cash corresponding to the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE is
promptly transferred to the STF at year-end.

We recommended that Management require the concerned Municipal Officials
and Employees to devise measures and exert more effort to expedite the preparation
and submission of the accounts and financial reports, including the supporting
documents and schedules, within the prescribed period. We request that such activities
be given utmost priority for timely and better fiscal reporting.

We recommended that Management:

a. Direct the GFPS and GAD key personnel to undergo capacity building
programs and training on GAD to enhance their knowledge and build their
competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender
mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting
and GAD-related tools like HGDG on the attributed budget to evaluate
gender responsiveness of PPAs in the GPB;

b. Ensure that the PPAs charged to GAD fund are only those that would
address the identified gender issues in the locality. The gender issues must
be specific and supported by evidence such as sex-disaggregated data and
gender analysis results; and

c. Direct the GFPS to update the GAD Code in compliance with the guidelines
set by the PCW.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Make immediate adjustment of financial records by recognizing the
completed Infrastructure Asset in the FS. A journal entry must be made by
debiting the Road Networks account and crediting the CIP – Infrastructure
Assets account, in the total amount of ₱196,599,868.68;

b. Implement stricter internal controls over the recording and classification of
infrastructure assets. This may involve establishing clear procedures for
asset verification, project completion documentation, timely and proper
recording of transactions pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for
LGUs. A checklist system for project completion could be particularly
helpful; and

c. Make proper disclosure of accounting errors in the Notes to FS, explaining
the affected accounts in the FS.

To rectify these deficiencies, we recommended that Management require the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Conduct a thorough review of all accounts affected by errors related to the
recoupment of mobilization fees, adjustments for withholding taxes, and
underpayment of MF to contractors. This review must include the
identification, detailed documentation, and reconciliation of all affected
accounts to ensure accuracy and compliance with accounting standards;

b. Credit ₱37,196,798.07 to the Advances to Contractors account to comply
with the PRDP Operations Manual, which mandates full recoupment of
advances upon reaching the 80 percent progress payment threshold (project
completed 100 percent on February 12, 2024); and

c. Implement proper internal controls and procedures to prevent similar
errors in the future.

To address the deficiencies related to the lack of documentation, we
recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Submit the lacking documents, in original copy or duly certified copy from
the original, the Certificate of Completion and Statement of Work
Accomplished, that will serve as reference in our audit actions;

b. Regularly communicate with the concerned personnel, such as the MPMIU
or PRDP focal person to follow up deficiencies in documentations to provide
basis for payment and minimize risks associated with incomplete
verification processes; and

c. Ensure that all essential documents are complete and properly attached in
original form prior to facilitating payment of transactions.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the MT, Municipal Project Management and Implementing Unit
(MPMIU) and all other concerned, to ensure timely submission of PRDP FS, reports,
DVs, JEVs, and supporting documentation, in accordance with existing rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention in the preparation and submission of the required BRS, review the records relevant to the Cash in Bank account, investigate the causes of abnormal balances of the said account and make the necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the FS and other documents;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Source Agencies on the status of fund transfers to the Municipality and facilitate the closure of the accounts that have become dormant in accordance with relevant laws, rules and regulations; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts to come up with the correct balances thereof as presented in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the status of projects recorded under the CIP accounts, reconcile their respective records and reclassify the completed projects to its appropriate PPE accounts duly supported with Certificates of Project Completion. We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to conduct an in-depth verification of the Due to BIR and Due to GSIS accounts, perform reconciliation of records, make necessary adjusting entries and remit immediately the withheld funds due to the BIR and GSIS.

We recommended that the LCE:
Consider hiring additional competent staffs and personnel in the Accounting Office to hasten the process of preparation and submission of monthly, quarterly and annual financial reports to the Office of the Auditor; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer to be mindful of the deadlines and adhere with the aforementioned provisions regarding the submission of trial balances, FS, SAAOB, disbursement and collection accounts and other supporting schedules.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to:
Ensure that prescribed planning and budgeting tools are prepared, submitted and effectively utilized and the timelines in the planning and budgeting process are strictly observed as prescribed under the aforementioned Circulars; Strictly comply with the requirements of the said JCs in identifying the PPAs that reflect an equitable distribution of the mandated priority expenditures for an effective utilization of funds and full attainment of its objectives; and stop the practice of lump-sum appropriation and identify the specific PPAs with specific amounts during budget preparation;
Ensure that planned projects/programs/activities for SEF are optimally implemented/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents in accordance with the proper utilization of the fund; and
Stop the charging and payment of MOOE which are not included in the list of expenses chargeable against the SEF and ensure that the SEF be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and its addenda.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the supporting documents are proper and complete before processing the claim or payment of the same.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 requiring the Municipal Accountant to submit future disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to the Auditor within the first 10 days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned officials to submit the lacking documents supporting the payments of the 1st and 2nd progress billings and the disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents of the 3rd progress billings for further audit evaluation.

We recommended that the LCE:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the remittance of the Due to BIR balance amounting to ₱2,324,749.88 as soon as possible;
Require the Municipal Accountant to review the provisions of the PRDP Financial Management Operation Guidelines and to carefully analyze future transaction relative to PRDP for the prevention of the same mistake; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of funds between the Loan proceeds, the GOP counterpart and the LGU Equity counterpart to reflect the accurate bank account balances.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the entries made for the progress billings of the PRDP Subproject and make necessary adjusting entries to reflect the appropriate balances of the CIP – Infrastructure Assets and Cash in Bank – LCCA accounts;
Make representations with the Contractor to reconcile the payments of the Municipality and effect appropriate adjustments to determine the amounts payable on the next progress billing or the final billing; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and other officers concerned to review the provisions of the PRDP Financial Management Operation Guidelines and the approved IMA to ensure compliance and to avoid incurrence of similar deficiencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of accounts and prepare adjusting entries to the affected accounts including the Advances to Contractor to reflect their appropriate balances.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office review the provisions of the PRDP Operations Manual, particularly on the recoupment of mobilization fee, to avoid similar errors in the future transactions of similar nature.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the management direct the
following officers to:
The Municipal Accountant:
1. Compute or trace any discrepancy with the RPCPPE and prepare proper adjusting
entries to correct the same; and,
2. Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner, through proper
time management and sound internal control, in order to achieve a fair presentation of
financial statements.
The General Services Officer:
1. Prepare the IIRUP as directed by MNGAS for LGU, Section 79 of PD 1445, and Section
379 of RA 7160, and facilitate the disposal of any unserviceable properties determined
therein;
2. Conduct annual physical inventory of all supplies and properties of the local government
unit as of December 31 of each year as mandated by the pertinent regulations; and,
3. Maintain and monitor Property Cards (PC) in order to have a sound internal control over
the monitoring of the PPEs.
We also recommend that the management start the formation of the Inventory Committee in
order to facilitate the conduct of the “One Time Cleansing” operation of the Municipality in
accordance with COA Circular 2020-006.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the management direct the
Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2,
1996, to submit the BRS including the bank statements, paid checks, original copies of
debit/credit memos within 15 days of the ensuing month as the prescribed period. Moreover,
coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to apply or register the accounts of the Municipality with
the respective depository banks’ websites which permit the generation of the statement of
accounts electronically at any point in time, when needed, or the WE-Access of the LBP.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the management direct the
Municipal Accountant to Coordinate with the source agency pertaining to the reversion of the
unexpended balance and/or make a representation for any possible reprogramming of the said
balances.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the management address the
causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs
and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social,
economic, and environmental development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine
Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the management:
1. Adopt the ATM Payroll System and coordinate with the nearest AGDB for faster
transactions, since LBP Midsayap is farther than the recently opened LBP Libungan;
and,
2. Communicate with the employees and express the advantage and disadvantages of the
system, and require them to cooperate with the enrollment process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the RHU to install strong internal control in
the RHU such as requiring pre-assessment of dental health patients and proper endorsement of
patients prior to making payments in the MTO or to the collecting officer thereby ensuring
efficient health services are duly rendered to the public.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the RHU to properly adhere to the Local
Revenue Code in determining the amount of fees to be charged for the health services rendered
and any other pertinent laws and regulations connected therewith. The Accountable Officer
should also be advised to have proper knowledge on the present laws and regulations which
directly involves their accountability.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned personnel to conduct complete annual physical count of PPE, reconcile the Property and Accounting records after the physical count and submit to the Auditor the RPCPPE for verification in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant must properly maintain Property Cards and PPELC and conduct periodic reconciliation of property and accounting records to come up with accurate reports.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances between the subsidiary ledgers and the balance per financial statement and give preferential attention on the complete preparation and submission of the BRSs pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Municipal Treasurer to transfer the balance of the account maintained at PNB to an AGDB, and to close accounts that have become dormant and revert back to the Source Agency or the Bureau of the Treasury, as the case maybe, if the balances represent excess of the implemented PPAs.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately all collections intact and in full and to adhere strictly to the guidelines relative to collections and deposits as required under Section 32 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
We likewise reiterated that the Municipal Treasurer must ensure that collections from various collectors are remitted daily to the Liquidating Officers and deposited regularly to the AGDB. The Liquidating Officer must verify the unused accountable forms in the possession of collecting clerks upon remittance to see to it that all collections are remitted daily and intact. The new booklet of accountable forms must only be issued upon full consumption of the whole stub to prevent the collecting clerks from retaining huge amount of collections in their custody.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to prepare a well-planned/studied development PPAs under the 20 percent DF that can be implemented immediately in order to optimize utilization and bring the desired benefits to its constituents and avoid future reversion/realignment of appropriation;
Ensure that development projects included under the 20 percent DF are those that are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, and those which are essential to promotion of the general welfare of the people; and
See to it that all development projects implemented by the Municipal government are closely monitored by MPDO to ensure that government funds are utilized for the intended purpose. Progress/periodic reports must also be prepared to validate actual existence and accomplishment of programs/projects being implemented.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to ensure that RDS are intact, details are complete and submitted to the Accounting Office to support the distribution of relief goods; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Accounting Staff to conduct thorough review and ensure completeness of supporting documents before effecting any payments in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require all drivers and concerned officials to completely fill out the Driver’s Trip Ticket in accordance with existing regulation for transparency and accountability over the use of government motor vehicles; and
Prepare and submit Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report, together with the list of vehicles and heavy equipment owned by the Municipality, to the Commission on Audit (thru the Municipal Accountant) for verification and evaluation  within 10 days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that Management properly utilize the transferred funds for projects and activities for which the funds were intended. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant must include in the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF funds transferred intended for rehabilitation and post-disaster recovery to ensure proper monitoring thereof.

We recommended that the VMO and SB stop the practice of granting assistance to Barangays in the form of distribution of bags of cement regardless of source of funds as it is not among its mandated powers, duties and functions under Sections 445 and 447 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that the PPAs for each year on SEF are implemented/utilized for the efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the constituents in accordance with the proper utilization of the fund;
Include in the Income and Expenditure Estimates the previous years’ unexpended balances as well as the continuing appropriation in determining the net amount available for appropriation; and
Regularly monitor the mechanism employed in assessing the rate of progress of program and project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the paid DVs on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office all the DVs and official receipts including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month; and

The IAR must also be properly filled-up as proof that items delivered are in accordance with the specifications, terms and conditions, and within the period and at the place indicated in the PO.

We recommended that Management ensure that Supplemental AIP be prepared for future government grants requiring compliance with the regular budget process. Moreover, it is recommended that Management be more vigilant in observing proper planning and budgeting prior to utilization of government fund.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entry to adjust the balances of the affected accounts. Provide a complete list of serviceable municipal vehicles to ascertain the reliability of fuel consumption and for validation purposes.

The inability of the Municipal Engineering Office to prioritize the preparation and submission of the list to the Auditor.

observations.managementAction: Only few properties were transferred to General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer to cancel
the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original
Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the
Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed for the reversion of the stale
checks. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor and update the lists of those
unclaimed checks so that follow up can be made to the payees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original DVs and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to: A. Require inventory committee to
conduct the mandatory annual physical count of the property, plant and equipment and
submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Municipal Accountant as a basis for required
reconciliation to the books. Moreover, instruct the department heads to monitor their
properties for easy consolidation. B. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the records with the physical count and appropriate adjustments
should be made to account for the unserviceable, obsolete, and lost properties. C. Require the
Property and Accounting Unit to maintain complete property and equipment ledger cards and
property cards as required under NGAS Manual to ensure correctness and reliability of the
PPE Account balances. D. Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who would lead for
the disposal of those unserviceable properties. E. Establish an Office of General
Services/Property Officer who will focus on the properties of the LGU as well as the
preparation of the needed inventory reports.
observations.managementAction: The management already created Appraisal and Disposal Committee that will handle the disposal of its properties. However, they did not conduct the mandatory annual physical count and no reports were submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting journal
entries to take up the depreciation expenses for the current year, update the accumulated
depreciation accounts as well as record adjustments in the government equity account for
Prior Years’ depreciation expenses not previously recorded. Moreover, if possible, prepare
lapsing schedule for depreciation, to be updated regularly, to serve as guide in the
computation of annual depreciation.
observations.managementAction: On-going checking of properties being depreciated and related computation of depreciation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator to design projects, programs and activities to be more definite,
specific and measurable in order to ensure measurement of performance of the municipality
relative to the implementation of its developmental projects.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the department heads involved to:

a. Closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects of the
Municipality which connotes delayed contribution to the attainment of desirable
socio-economic and environmental outcomes and to maximize the usage of the fund;
b. Fast track the implementation based on the schedule time frame by the implementing
department concerned to achieve the desired management outcomes;
c. Determine the reason for non-implementation of some projects and evaluate the
possibility to make amendments to some projects which are not feasible for
implementation; and
d. Strict compliance with the provisions of JMC 2017-1 of DILG and DBM should be
observed.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that employees will receive no less
than the threshold prescribed in the General Appropriations Act in every Fiscal Year, ₱5,000.00
for the ensuing year 2018; evaluate requests for deductions against salaries for the payment of
loans and not permit if such will lower the employee's net take home pay below the
threshold, instead, advise employees to personally settle/directly remit dislodged payments for
the loans from private lending institutions over the counter.
observations.managementAction: There are still employees who have a net take home pay below the minimum requirement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the person determined liable for the
disallowances to settle/refund the said disallowances immediately in accordance with COA
Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Out of P95,670.00 disallowance, determined liable for P89,170.00 were settled as of December 31, 2018. Balance for 2018 were P6,500 only.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the local chief executive to: 1. Revisit, review and evaluate the
programs/project/activities included in the Annual Investment Plan of the 20% Economic
Development Fund to ensure effective formulation, implementation and utilization of the
Fund; 2. Ensure that all thus projects to be funded from the 20% Economic Development
Fund shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure; 3. Adhere strictly with the
provisions of DILG- DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 and 2017-01 dated April 13,
2011 and February 22, 2017, respectively, to ensure that 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized
to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcome; 4. Submit
the lacking documents and transactions enumerated in Annex B. Ensure 100% complete
submission of accounts and transactions by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Some of the 20% EDF still has an MOOE items instead of Capital Outlay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management to adhere strictly with the proper accounting and
reporting guidelines based on COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. In particular,
we recommend to: a. Transfer to the Special Trust Fund account under Trust Liabilities -
DRRMF-MOOE. b. Submit to the COA Auditor's Office the monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of the LDRRMF so that verification can be made on the said fund. c. Draw Journal
Entry to adjust the correct account for the acquisition of rescue equipment. d. Revisit, review
and evaluate the programs/project/activities included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund Investment Plant of the Municipality to ensure effective formulation,
implementation and utilization of LDRRM Fund; and e. Ensure strict utilization of LDRRM
funds for calamity-related activities so as to accomplish all planned projects and activities. f.
Submit resolutions of the local sanggunian declaring the LGU under the state of calamity or a
Presidential of state of calamity upon recommendation of NDRRMC for claims charged
against the Quick Response Fund. g. Submit the lacking documents and transactions
enumerated in Annex L. Ensure 100% complete submission of accounts and transaction by
the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: No report on utilization of LDRRM Fund was submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management exert their best effort to fully implement the
targeted priority development projects and programs embodied in the Annual Investment Plan
pursuant to its development mandate. Strictly administer and supervise the implementation
of development projects and likewise monitor its implementation. Determine the reason for
non-implementation of some projects and evaluate the possibility to make amendments to
some project which are not feasible for implementation.

The Management should ensure that the PPAs under the Mitigation Fund are provided with
details in the LDRRMFIP. Likewise the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the
LDRRMF be prepared and submitted to the COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Lists of PPAs under the Mitigation Fund and projects those under the Special Trust Fund and the Report of Sources and Utilization were prepared yet, but submission of the required reports were delayed due to minimum manpower assigned in the DRRM Office.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive should instruct the concerned official/personnel to: a.
Consolidate the PPMPs submitted by the end-user Offices; b. Implement strictly timelines on
the posting and submission of procurement reports; c. Establish procurement timelines in
consonance with project schedules, including a cut-off date for processing requisitions and
issuance of purchase orders to ensure receipt of most of the procured items in time for the
project implementation; d. Consider the strict observance of RA 9184 and its IRR, and other
existing rules and regulation on government procurement as well as imposition of control
measures to ensure that procurement is effected in an efficient, economical and effective
manner that is most advantageous to the government and to prevent possible disallowances
for such violation.
observations.managementAction: Lack of manpower to focus on the consolidation of CY 2016 PPMPs.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to restitute immediately the cash shortage of P3,425,442.29
as promised; relieve the Municipal Treasurer to avoid incurrence of additional shortage under
her accountability; withheld the salaries and other emoluments due the AO and apply same in
payment of indebtedness; and institute appropriate charges against the AO for the missing
funds.
observations.managementAction: Partial settlement made thru salary deduction of the Municipal Treasurer. Partial settlement had already been made.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned should undertake effective,
efficient and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection efficiency. Moreover,
we recommend that Sangguniang Bayan to enjoin to impose the legal remedies either by
administrative auction thru levy on real property or by judicial auction to enforce the
collections of the computed arrearages amounting to P146,575,868.28.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
The management should implement effective control system on the property management
through the following: a. The Local Chief Executive shall appoint a General Service Officer
that will be responsible in the maintenance and safekeeping of government properties, records,
and to ensure the systematic delivery of basic services to the people. b. The Local Chief
Executive shall require the periodic physical count of property annually and the
reconciliation between the accounting and property records after inventory taking. A report
thereat shall be made using the report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment
(RPCPPE) which shall be submitted to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the
ensuing year; and The designated GSO shall prepare and issue ARE, instead MR, which shall
be renewed every three (3) years; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly
reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasury Office to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the
certified list of taxpayers as basis in establishing the RPT Receivable.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of RPT records on the Treasury together with the Assessor’s records is still not yet done as to the taxable property.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive should instruct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO)
to prepare the payrolls while the Municipal Accountant ensure compliance to the
aforementioned provisions of GAA. We further recommended that management discontinue
the practice of deducting payments of monthly loan amortizations of private lending
establishments from the personnel’s salary and wages payroll. Those personnel whom have
loan agreement with private lending institutions must directly remit their payments to the
collecting agent/s or office of respective lending establishments.
observations.managementAction: There are still some employees who have a net take home pay below the minimum requirement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management immediately assess the causes of delays in the project
implementation and address all issues accordingly in order to fast track the implementation
of priority development projects. Municipal Planning Coordinator did not regularly monitor
thru submission of accomplishment reports on the status of development and other local
projects.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the local chief executive secure copies of PNDF Volume 1, current
requisitioning edition, and to direct the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) and other
requisitioning officers to supplement certification for each procurement transaction.
observations.managementAction: Concerned Municipal Health Officer (MHO) supplement certification for each procurement transaction. He made reference to safe medicines.

observations.recommendation:
Require the concerned accountable officers to settle the suspensions immediately and
refund the disallowances totalling 8,999,189.82 and 4,340.00, respectively. Suspensions not
settled within ninety days (90) days shall become a disallowance pursuant to the above-cited
provisions.
observations.managementAction: Out of this amount, only P3,760 for suspensions and P6,500 for the notice of disallowance remain.

observations.recommendation:
1. Institute measures to correct the inappropriate practices on semi-expendable supplies,
property, plant and equipment. 2. Create a Disposal Committee who shall oversee the
inspection, appraisal and disposal of unserviceable properties. Properties deemed
unserviceable must be disposed at any manner most appropriate. Application for disposal
must be attached by required documents. 3. Instruct the Property Officer to prepare Property
Acknowledgement Receipt for all items issued. 4. Renew PARs every three years after
issuance. On returned properties, the original copy of the MR/ARE must be given back to the
property officer for cancellation with the duplicate copy. 5. Notify immediately the auditor of
the occurrence of the loss. Conduct investigation and prepare report thereon. Within 30 days
submit an application for relief supported by the following documents including affidavits and
police report.

Exert effort to prove the authenticity of the liability. If unable, revert the unestablished
liability to Government Equity. Make certain, that the recorded payables are legal, and valid
claims against the agency.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office had made representation to verify the account with the NEA, however, was not responded to.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly comply with the procurement procedures as mandated under the Government
Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184) and the Memorandum of Agreement, to deter sanction/s
for violation/non-compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: No update on the compliance of other CY requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1. Require the Accountable Officers/Collection Officers to remit in full all their future collections and not retain portions of such during the turnover;

2. Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly enforce remittance of collections from the Accountable Officers/Collection Officers within the prescribed period to avoid incurrence of shortages and/or possible misuse of public funds; and

3. Require the Accounting Office to ensure regular reconciliation of balances and prepare subsidiary ledgers for all accountable officers to properly and correctly determine respective accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to conduct meeting with the AOs to address this deficiency. Management also promised to consider adoption of policy that will penalize the AOs for holding portion of collections.
observations.reason: Portion of collections of the AOs were retained. AOs disclosed that some collections were used to cater the advances for travel of some employees. No reconciliation of accounts were made because the Accounting Office did not maintain subsidiary ledger cards per accountable officers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
		
4. Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention to the preparation and submission of the required bank reconciliation statements considering that the variance had already accumulated to ₱13.55 million and to adjust accordingly any error and reconciling item discovered in the reconciliation process to arrive at the correct and reliable cash in bank balance; and

5. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to update religiously the cash books and the data reflected be preferably considered in the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted; and

6. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer all government funds from PNB to authorized government depository banks (AGDB), initiate the closure of the account in the said bank and communicate with the DBM what bank account the IRA must be downloaded, pursuant to DOF Department Circular No. 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to assign a personnel that will exclusively prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.reason: The preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements, the maintenance of updated cashbooks and the transfer of the government funds from PNB to the AGDB were given less priority due to other responsibilities of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled accounts.  Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to assign a personnel that will review the entries made on the reciprocal accounts and will propose adjusting entry/ies to correct the said accounts.
observations.reason: The review of the reciprocal accounts was not prioritize by the Municipal Accountant, this is due to various reasons such as the bulk of transactions for processing, other reports to be submitted and other responsibilities that were assigned to him.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to determine, once and for all, the collectability of these Receivable accounts together with the dormant cash advances and to document the request for write-off from the Commission on Audit on the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to conduct detailed review of these accounts to determine their collectability, and will initiate the write-of thereafter.
observations.reason: Facilitating the write-off of the dormant accounts was not prioritize by Management.

observations.managementAction: Management promised to require the conduct of inventory-taking. Management also promised to consider the creation of the General Services Office (GSO)
observations.reason: The creation of GSO was not considered due to budget consideration. The Head of the Inventory Committee disclosed that the inventory-taking was not finished, thus, no report was prepared and submitted. The maintenance of ledger cards was not prioritize by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

19. Direct the Municipal Treasury Office to implement collection strategies to further improve revenue assessment and collection.  Periodic collection performance and monitoring must be conducted to ensure efficient collection of projected income;

19. Instruct Municipal Treasurer or her authorized representative to examine the books, accounts, and other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association subject to local taxes, fees and charges in order to ascertain, assess, and collect the correct amount of the tax, fee, or charge; and

20. Direct the Local Finance Committee or the Municipal Treasurer, Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer to formulate realistic income projections based on Statistics of past years’ experience and profile of local taxpayers and devise effective plan to avoid, mitigate and control the risks of possible non-collection of projected incomes.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to implement collection strategies to improve the collection performance.
observations.reason: Some provisions in the Municipal Tax Code were not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasury Office to implement collection strategies to further improve revenue assessment and collection. We also recommended that the Sanguniang Bayan devise an ordinance that will assist the Treasury Office in assessing the basis for CTC tax, such as requiring the CTC taxpayers to file a sworn declaration of total earnings derived from business, compensation, exercise of profession and income from real property during the preceding year.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to implement a collection campaign to increase the collection of the Community Tax.
observations.reason: Due to the laxity of Treasury personnel in assessing the correct amount of tax.

observations.recommendation: 22. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive discuss with the Treasury and Budget Office the strict implementation of Sections 23 and 24 of the Resolution No. 16, Series of 2010 or the Solid Waste Management Ordinance, and devise strategies to improve the collection of revenues including those for the Mandatory Polluters Fee and the SWM fees and charges. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discuss with the Budget Officer the inclusion of a line item for Garbage Fees or Polluters Fee in the projected income of the Annual Budget.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to implement the provisions of the Municipal Tax Code relating to the garbage fees.
observations.reason: The implementation of garbage fees collection was not prioritize by Management.

observations.recommendation: 26. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials and employees to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of perfected contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review, the results of which will be used in post-audit of disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to conduct meeting with BAC and discuss this deficiency.
observations.reason: Submission of the copies of contracts, POs and their supporting documents was not prioritized by Management.

observations.recommendation: 38. We recommended that Management require the concerned employee to submit copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA TAS.
observations.managementAction: The Management will require the BAC to submit the copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documentation to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA TAS.
observations.reason: Due to lack of coordination between the BAC, Municipal Engineering Office and other implementing offices.

observations.recommendation: 46. We recommended that management require the concerned personnel to submit copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA TAS.
observations.managementAction: Submission of purchase orders and perfected contracts within five days upon perfection.
observations.reason: Due to lack of coordination between the BAC, Municipal Engineering Office and other implementing offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to: a. Adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for the supplies and materials provided under the NGAS Manual; b. All purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as inventories under the appropriate account; and c. Review properly the accounting entries and the account use to avoid committing similar errors.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Not Implemented The management still recognized the purchased inventories as part of the expense. No reports were also submitted to the Accountant for adjustments made at year end.
observations.reason: The management still recognized the purchased inventories as part of the expense. No reports were also submitted to the Accountant for adjustments made at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should maintain separate books for the special accounts in depositories in accordance with the provisions of Chapter 6, Volume I of NGAS so that management would be informed on the financial position and condition of each special account as of a given time to guide them in taking significant actions to boost the operation of each account. Further, require the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution properly authorizing the LGU to open a separate depository accounts of the said special accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 Not Implemented Special accounts were also lumped to the General Fund accounts. Moreover, depositories of these special accounts were also not opened.
observations.reason: Special accounts were also lumped to the General Fund accounts. Moreover, depositories of these special accounts were also not opened.

observations.recommendation: The Management should ensure all real estate of the LGU are registered under the Torrens Title System and responsible for their proper and management.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 13, Part II, AAR CY 2016 Not implemented LGU did not secure a copy of their Title of their ownership of land.
observations.reason: LGU did not secure a copy of their Title of their ownership of land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require that an actual inventory-taking of all assets or PPEs of the LGU be conducted each year pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs, that the relevant RPCPPE be submitted not later than January 31 of each year to the Office of the Auditor and that the same be reconciled with the records of the Accountant.
We also recommended that Management consider creating a General Services Office (GSO) that will discharge on a full time basis the procurement and issuance of supplies and property; maintain updated Property Cards per category on all Agency’s assets as recorded in the list of PPE of accounting records; and facilitate the proper conduct of the physical count and preparation of the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention on the preparation and submission of the required BRS, pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011, and prepare subsidiary ledgers per bank accounts; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer:
To update religiously the cash books and the data reflected be preferably considered in the preparation of the BRS to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted; and
To transfer all government funds from PNB to authorized government banks, initiate the closure of the account in the said bank and communicate with the DBM what bank account the IRA must be downloaded to.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant:
To conduct a detailed review of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amount and make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts; and
To exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Collecting Officers to turnover or remit all collections daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer in compliance with Sections 8 and 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382;
Direct the Liquidating Officer to closely supervise the Collecting Officers and monitor their daily collections and remittances; and
Strengthen internal control over collection and remittances to avoid holding big amount of money in the hands of the Collecting Officers and consider the following:
Promulgate and implement a policy on the turnover of daily collections by the Collecting Officers to the Liquidating Officer. A cut-off may be established on the time of turnover that will give the Liquidating Officer enough time to deposit the turned-over collections; and
Promulgate and implement a policy that will penalize holding of collections for several days in the hands of the Collecting Officers.

We recommended that Management exert extra efforts to conduct verification, analysis and validation of these receivable balances, to determine once and for all their collectability and pursue collection, refund or liquidation thereof, if warranted. We also recommended to document the request for write-off from the Commission on Audit, duly supported with the required documents, the account balances which are considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant, the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO), Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to review all available documents relative to LDRRMF and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the financial statements and other documents;
Instruct the aforementioned officers to revisit and strictly follow the provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 10121, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC)-DBM-Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of Agency’s unutilized LDRRMF to the special TF by year of transfer, receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency, LDRRMF from other LGUs by LGU and other sources by donor.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to initiate the transfer of the unutilized balance of LDRRMF in the GF, at the end of the year, to the TF-LDRRM bank account;
If no actual cash transfer is made at the end of the year, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries in the books of GF and TF, to wit:
Direct the LDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP pursuant to the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002, showing the amount appropriated for MOOE and Capital Outlay.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasury Office to review the provisions of the Municipality’s Tax Code and implement collection strategies to further improve revenue assessment and collection.  Periodic collection performance and monitoring must be conducted to ensure efficient collection of projected income;
Direct Municipal Treasurer or his authorized representative to examine the books, accounts, and other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association subject to local taxes, fees and charges in order to ascertain, assess, and collect the correct amount of the tax, fee, or charge; and
Direct the Local Finance Committee to formulate realistic income projections based on Statistics of past years’ experience and profile of local taxpayers and devise effective plan to avoid, mitigate and control the risks of possible non-collection of projected incomes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Supply Officer to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of all its government vehicles subjected to repairs;
Require that the Certificate of Acceptance and RWM be prepared by the Supply Officer and approved by the Local Chief Executive; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain ledger cards for the expenses incurred regarding repairs and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to monitor whether the property is economically repairable or not.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to strictly observe the applicable provisions in the procurement of drugs and laboratory supplies. We also recommended that Management instruct the Accounting Office to strengthen their controls by requiring adequate documentation on each transaction involving procurement of drugs and medicines before payment is effected.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to secure immediately the needed documents to facilitate the timely preparation of the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit a copy thereof to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, pursuant to the procedures of the aforecited Circular, to facilitate verification thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials and employees to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of perfected copies of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days after perfection for a timely and complete conduct of auditorial and technical review.
We also directed the attention of Management to Section 4.0, the Penalty Clause, of COA Circular No. 2009-001, which states that the inability of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements of the same must be subject to administrative disciplinary action and the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents may be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the SWM Focal Person to prioritize the approval of the MSWMP that will ensure the efficient management of solid wastes generated within the Municipality’s jurisdiction; and organize the SWMB, compliant with prescribed composition enumerated in Section 16 of RA No. 9003, that will adapt measures to promote the effective implementation of the SWM programs.

We recommended that Management implement immediately the PPAs pertinent to the unutilized balance of ₱17.68 million as embodied in the Annual Investment Program (AIP). We also recommended that Management judiciously plan the PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent LDF and ensure its full utilization for the benefit of the constituents and local government itself, in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-001 dated February 22, 2017.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to create an inventory committee
that will spearhead the conduct of inventory taking on all the properties of the LGU
and require them to prepare the RPCPPE and submit the said report to the Office of the
Auditor. Moreover, instruct all department heads to monitor and verify the properties
belonging to their offices for easy consolidation of the inventory committee.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned
Department Heads to require them to maintain the accurate property records for easy
monitoring and validation of the properties of the LGU.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Properties be prepared and accomplished by the Inspection, Appraisal and Disposal
Committee. Proper and necessary disposal of unserviceable properties be undertaken
in accordance with the provisions of Section 79 of PD 1445. Further, the Municipal
Accountant shall adhere to the provisions of PPSAS No. 17 on the derecognition of
the carrying amount of the PPE items.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw journal entry to transfer
all the completed assets under Trust Fund to General Fund. Moreover, take up
provision for depreciation on the accounts cited above so that correct valuation of the
accounts in the financial statements will be reported.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned
department heads to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements set forth
under the COA Circular 2015-008. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to
provide depreciation of each component of the road network system for the correct
recording of the said account in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the rules and
procedures in accounting for supplies and materials provided under the PPSAS 12 and
NGAS Manual. All purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as
inventories under the appropriate account and review properly the accounting entries
and the account use to avoid committing similar errors.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant
with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the
preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed for the
reversion of the stale checks. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor
and update the lists of those unclaimed checks so that follow up can be made to the
payees.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the management to require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the concerned servicing banks for the timely submission of the Bank
Statement with supporting documents and to prepare/submit the BRS to the Office of
the Auditor within the required period for review. Necessary adjusting or correcting
entries must be prepared immediately by the Municipal Accountant for proper
