observations.reason: Due to lack of coordination between the BAC, Municipal Engineering Office and other implementing offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) and Local Development Council (LDC) to prepare the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the formats prescribed in Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002. This must be certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submitted on or before the 15th day after end of each month to the assigned to COA Audit Team. 

We further recommended that the unutilized portion of the LDRRMF must be deposited in full to the Trust Fund Account per Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Management will require the LDRRM Officer and Local Development Council to prepare the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed formats in Annex A and B of COA Circular No. 2012-002
observations.reason: Preparation of the report was not given priority due to voluminous transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer/Municipal Planning and Development Officer provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going and to be implemented PPAs during the year to monitor the status of implementation of each infrastructure project.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer/MPDO to regularly coordinate with the Audit Team of the necessary information or reports that might be needed during the course of the audit of these PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
Management will require the Municipal Engineer/Municipal Planning and Development Office to provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year a list of all on-going and to be implemented PPAs during the year to monitor the status of implementation of each infrastructure projects.

The Municipal Mayor will direct the Municipal Engineer/MPDO to regularly coordinate with the Audit Team of the necessary information reports that might be needed during the course of the Audit of the PPAs.
observations.reason: The concerned officers overlooked their responsibility to provide the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going programs/projects/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the General Services Officer to submit copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders and their complete supporting documents to the Auditor within five (5) days after their perfection for purposes of review of said contracts and technical evaluation by COA TAS.
observations.managementAction: Submission of purchase orders and perfected contracts within five days upon perfection.
observations.reason: Due to lack of coordination between the BAC, Municipal Engineering Office and other implementing offices.
The LCE issued a memorandum to the Municipal Treasurer for the conduct of the physical inventory yet no physical count was conducted and no report was prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management should implement effective control system on the property management through the following: a. The Local Chief Executive shall appoint a General Service Officer that will be responsible in the maintenance and safekeeping of government properties, records, and to ensure the systematic delivery of basic services to the people. b. The Local Chief Executive shall require the periodic physical count of property annually and the reconciliation between the accounting and property records after inventory taking. A report thereat shall be made using the report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be submitted to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the ensuing year; and The designated GSO shall prepare and issue ARE, instead MR, which shall be renewed every three (3) years; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.reason: Organizational Chart is still on-going, hence, limited manpower were assigned. Still, no inventory taking were made by the LGU to its PPE accounts. Also, reports were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: The Management should ensure all real estate of the LGU are registered under the Torrens Title System and responsible for their proper and effective use and management.
observations.reason: LGU did not secure a copy of their ownership of land.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to: a. Resort to NFA as primary supplier or source of rice in all its procurements whenever it grants prices rice as a form of incentive or benefit to their employees or use rice in connection with their functions; and b. Forge with the NFA an appropriate Memorandum of Agreement to facilitate and operationalize the rice procurement arrangement.
observations.reason: Management is still planning to secure MOA with NFA for the said rice procurement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Accountable Officers/Collection Officers to remit in full all their future collections and not retain portions of such during the turnover;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly enforce remittance of collections from the Accountable Officers/Collection Officers within the prescribed period to avoid incurrence of shortages and/or possible misuse of public funds; and
Require the Accounting Office to ensure regular reconciliation of balances and prepare subsidiary ledgers for all accountable officers to properly and correctly determine respective accountabilities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention to the preparation and submission of the required bank reconciliation statements considering that the variance had already accumulated to ₱13.55 million and to adjust accordingly any error and reconciling item discovered in the reconciliation process to arrive at the correct and reliable cash in bank balance; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer:
To update religiously the cash books and the data reflected be preferably considered in the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted; and
To transfer all government funds from PNB to authorized government banks, initiate the closure of the account in the said bank and communicate with the DBM what bank account the IRA must be downloaded, pursuant to DOF Department Circular No. 001-2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled accounts.  Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare an adjusting journal entry to correct the balances of Loans Payable and Government Equity, beginning, due to erroneous recording of principal payments and non-recording of GRT in the books;
Strengthen accounting controls over loans payable by reviewing thoroughly the amortization schedule given by the bank and carefully analyzing transaction before recording in the books and updating entries of both records; and
Ask for a copy of Billing Statement or Debit memo from the bank monthly regarding the automatic deduction for Loan Payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to determine, once and for all, the collectability of these Receivable accounts together with the dormant cash advances and to document the request for write-off from the Commission on Audit on the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Accordingly, request authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the dormant accounts duly supported with the required documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Strengthen the control over the cash advances of the Agency, relating to Confidential and Intelligence Fund, by strictly conforming to the documentary requirements enumerated in the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Memorandum Circular 2015-001 and pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1445, before disbursing funds;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Disbursement Vouchers together with the prescribed documentary requirements pertaining to Cash Advance for Confidential and Intelligence Fund within seven (7) working days from the release of the checks; and
Ensure that the cash advances are granted to the designated Special Disbursing Officer within his maximum cash accountability and strictly granted and liquidated in accordance with the provisions of aforecited joint memorandum circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasury Office to implement collection strategies to further improve revenue assessment and collection.  Periodic collection performance and monitoring must be conducted to ensure efficient collection of projected income;
Instruct Municipal Treasurer or her authorized representative to examine the books, accounts, and other pertinent records of any person, partnership, corporation, or association subject to local taxes, fees and charges in order to ascertain, assess, and collect the correct amount of the tax, fee, or charge; and
Direct the Local Finance Committee or the Municipal Treasurer, Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Officer to formulate realistic income projections based on Statistics of past years’ experience and profile of local taxpayers and devise effective plan to avoid, mitigate and control the risks of possible non-collection of projected incomes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasury Office to implement collection strategies to further improve revenue assessment and collection. We also recommended that the Sanguniang Bayan devise an ordinance that will assist the Treasury Office in assessing the basis for CTC tax, such as requiring the CTC taxpayers to file a sworn declaration of total earnings derived from business, compensation, exercise of profession and income from real property during the preceding year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive discuss with the Treasury and Budget Office the strict implementation of Sections 23 and 24 of the Resolution No. 16, Series of 2010 or the Solid Waste Management Ordinance, and devise strategies to improve the collection of revenues including those for the Mandatory Polluters Fee and the SWM fees and charges. We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to discuss with the Budget Officer the inclusion of a line item for Garbage Fees or Polluters Fee in the projected income of the Annual Budget.

We recommended that the Management:
Judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities for the Solid Waste Management, allocate amounts per PPA listed and promptly implement them as embodied in the plan;
Direct the Solid Waste Management Board to require the other 33 barangays of the Municipality to allocate a certain parcel of land through their Sanggunian for the establishment of MRF; and
Strictly implement the Resolution No. 15 for the closure of the open dumpsite and establish sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 41 and 42 of Republic Act 9003 to protect the constituents’ public health and safety.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal officials and employees to strictly comply with the mandatory submission of perfected contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for a timely and complete conduct of Auditorial and technical review, the results of which will be used in post-audit of disbursements.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
A. Require inventory committee to conduct the mandatory annual physical count of the
property, plant and equipment and submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor
copy furnished the Municipal Accountant as a basis for reconciliation to the books.
B. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to reconcile the records with the
physical count and appropriate adjustments should be made to account for the
unserviceable, obsolete, and lost properties.
C. Require the Property and Accounting Unit to maintain complete property and
equipment ledger cards and property cards as required under NGAS Manual to ensure
correctness and reliability of the PPE Account balances.
D. Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who would lead for the disposal of
those unserviceable properties.
E. Establish an Office of General Services/Property Officer who will focus on the
properties of the LGU as well as the preparation of the needed inventory reports.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Enjoin the concerned department heads to comply with the requirements of COA
Circular 2015-008 and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team for
evaluation and verification.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry for the transfer of the Local
Road Network from Registry of Public Infrastructure in accordance with the above
mentioned provision of the COA Circular.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting journal
entries to take up the depreciation expenses for the current year, update the related accumulated
depreciation accounts as well as record adjustments in the government equity account for Prior
Years’ depreciation expenses not previously recorded. Moreover, if possible, prepare lapsing
schedule for depreciation, to be updated regularly, to serve as guide in the computation of annual
depreciation.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for
supplies and materials provided under the NGAS Manual;
b. All purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as inventories under the
appropriate account; and
c. Review properly the accounting entries and the account use to avoid committing similar
errors.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts,
prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct amount balances of
both accounts in order to present reliable data on the LGU’s financial position as of the given
period of time.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the local government unit discontinue the practice of allocating
lump-sum, discretionary funds to the members of Sangguniang Bayan in its Annual Budget.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should maintain separate books for
the special accounts in accordance with the provisions of Chapter 6, Volume I of NGAS so
that management would be informed on the financial position and condition of each special
account as of a given time to guide them in taking significant actions to boost the operation of
each account. Further, we recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan shall pass a resolution
properly authorizing the LGU to open a separate depository accounts of the said special
accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator to design projects, programs and activities to be more definite,
specific and measurable in order to ensure measurement of performance of the municipality
relative to the implementation of its developmental projects.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the department heads involved
to:
a. Closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects for the
attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes
and to maximize the usage of the fund;
b. Fast track the implementation based on the schedule time frame by the implementing
department concerned to achieve the desired management outcomes;
c. Determine the reason for non-implementation of some projects and evaluate the
possibility to make amendments to some projects which are not feasible for
implementation;
d. Strict compliance with the provisions of JMC 2017-1 of DILG and DBM should be
observed.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Inventory Committee and the General Services Officer to conduct a complete count of the Municipality’s PPE and prepare the Report as provided by Section 57, Volume II of NGAs Manual and submit the same to the Local Chief Executive and Auditor;

Require the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to maintain records on all properties of the agency; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain ledger cards of all properties. This should be reconciled with stock/property cards maintained by the GSO or Municipal Treasurer
observations.managementAction: Management will submit a properly accomplished inventory report and to maintain ledger cards of all its properties in CY 2019 onwards.
observations.reason:
The chairman of the inventory committee was unable to discharge her task since she underwent a three-month rehabilitation after she came across a vehicular accident in mid-2018. During the period she was on leave, the inventory committee members have started conducting the count. However, they were not able to come up with the RCPPE because of the time constraints.

The Municipal Accountant prepares a lapsing schedule which contains all the PPE procured since the beginning. The entries made therein reconciled/tallied in the amount presented in the PPE account on the statement of financial position. Because of the voluminous transactions in the office and lack of personnel to carry out the entries in the PPE Ledger Cards that these were not maintained in the required format.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management exercise utmost diligence in the prompt submission of Disbursement Vouchers to the Office of the Auditor complete with their respective supporting documents. The Audit Team also suggested that the Management assess their internal control in the disbursement of fund for any practice not to be tolerated so as to prevent further occurrence of this deficiency in the succeeding financial years.

We also reminded Management and the concerned officials and employees of the administrative or criminal liability, under Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445, Section 23 (1) (3) of the Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, which may be imposed as a consequence of their inability or refusal to render accounts without justifiable cause.
observations.reason: The delay of submission in 2017 was caused by the Municipal Treasury’s inability to submit timely the DVs and RCDs to the Municipal Accounting Office on the prescribed period

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management:

Strictly enforce the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2017; 

Stop granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; and

Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring   accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has already stopped granting additional cash advances to employees who still have unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Majority of the remaining unliquidated cash advances pertains to CAs made by the previous Municipal Treasurers and that of Former Mayor. Only minimal amount represents that of the employees currently employed in the LGU and majority of the CAs were drawn on December 2017` which were liquidated accordingly in the ensuring year.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the noted deficiencies, we recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant submit the Disbursement Vouchers pertaining to Cash Advance for Confidential and Intelligence Fund within seven (7) working days from the release of check. In addition, the said DVs must be placed in a sealed envelope with a visible label “CONFIDENTIAL-For the COA ATL Only” and with a covering transmittal letter enumerating the submission; and

The Municipal Mayor direct the concerned official to process or make some follow up to COA Central Office with regard to the Credit Notice of liquidations for the past 8 years including the current year. The said liquidations are not yet reflected in the Financial Statements as expense, thus still form part of the outstanding Cash Advances of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office will provide the supporting documents to the Auditor.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accounting Office submitted the DVs made for CAs pertaining to Confidential and Intelligence Expenses to the Office of the Auditor. Although the documentation are complete upon submission of the liquidation to the COA Central Office, they are amenable that they did not attach the same documents to the CAs which they submitted to the field auditor.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended to:

See to it that the 20 per cent Development Fund be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefit of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and

Implement immediately the programs and projects pertinent to the unutilized balance of ₱ 1.7 million as mentioned above.

We recommended that Management:

Require the contractor to undertake immediate completion of the projects otherwise, take appropriate remedies as provided in the Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184 including the computation and payment of liquidated damages;

See to it that all concerned offices are involved in the planning phase of any program/project/activity of the Municipality to effectively deliver them to the Constituents of Cawayan; and

Ensure that all salient provisions are incorporated in the contract entered into by the Municipality for all its undertakings for an effective and efficient implementation of the same.

We recommended that Management must observe the proper guidelines with regard to procurement of service vehicles. An authority to purchase from the Secretary of DILG must be secured by the local government before disbursing any government fund relating to service vehicle acquisition. The documents needed to be able to acquire approval are enumerated in this writing. Attached herewith is the checklist of requirements in the issuance of authority to purchase of motor vehicle (Annex A) for your reference.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the preparation of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan as required under Section 16 of RA 9003. This plan will serve as a tool in achieving the objectives of the Management with regard to Solid Waste Management. To be included in this plan are policies to be adopted by the Municipality like, “no segregation, no collection”.  We further recommend that the said plan be properly implemented to ensure the efficient management of solid waste generated within its jurisdiction.

We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better ways of performing its task. Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01, as mentioned above, serve as the proper way of structuring the GAD Focal Point System. More projects and activities could be implemented, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, the Agency must submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the COA Audit Team assigned within 5 working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the PCW. Likewise, the Audit Team must be furnished with a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report within 5 working days from the end of January of the ensuing year.

We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the trust collections due to other government agencies. Prompt remittance of trust collections to other government agencies concerned must be observed to allow the latter of the timely use of these resources for productive economic programs and activities.

We recommended that Management intensify its tax collection drive by sending regularly billings/ notice to taxpayers and making personal follow-ups constantly on the delinquent ones. The Municipality must avail of the legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Section 256, 257, and 258 of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Management will intensify its tax collection drive by sending regular billings/notices to the taxpayers and making personal follow-ups constantly on the delinquent ones.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasury’s insufficient travel fund is one of the reasons why there was inefficient collections of RPT and SET. The collectors cannot collect from the far flung barangays because they do not have enough travel allowances to sustain the expenses needed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management inform the public of the projects implemented by the LGU through signboards posted in conspicuous places within the agency premises, and in the venue where the PPA is located or carried out regardless of amount or source of funds to enhance transparency in the implementation of government projects.

We also recommended to follow the right specifications of the signboard as exemplified above in Section 2.2.3 of COA Circular 2013-004.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirement for the prompt submission of the monthly reports together with disbursement vouchers/payrolls/official receipts and their supporting papers to apprise the Chief Executive about the financial status of the Municipality needed for sound decision making and as means of improving performance efficiency. Prompt submission of these reports/accounts are required for timely review by the Auditor and early detection of error or deficiencies. 

Further, we reminded the Agency to see to it that checks are issued/released to creditor only if disbursement vouchers are duly processed and signed by the proper authorities.

We reiterated compliance to the audit recommendations offered in the previous audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of conducting physical count of LGU properties and submitting updated RPCPPE to the auditor concerned.
observations.managementAction: Conduct Inventory Count
observations.reason: See Observation No. 1 of Part III

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to strictly monitor the effectivity and expiry dates of fidelity bonds of their Accountable Officers and see to it that renewal be made month before their expiry. No Accountable Officers with expired bonds be allowed to continue performing their duties and responsibilities. The Municipal Treasurer should also be instructed to submit to COA every 1st week of January every year, a list of Bonded Officials and Accountable Officers.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to strictly monitor the effectivity and expiry dates of fidelity bonds of their Accountable Officers and see to it that renewal be made month before their expiry. No Accountable Officers with expired bonds be allowed to continue performing their duties and responsibilities. The Municipal Treasurer should also be instructed to submit to COA every 1st week of January every year, a list of Bonded Officials and Accountable Officers.

We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better ways of performing its task. The above mentioned guidelines serve as the proper way of structuring the GAD Focal Point System. More projects and activities could be implemented, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, GFPS shall submit the agency’s   GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB shall be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. Actual GAD projects and activities should also be aligned with the GAD plan per COA Circular 14-001 and Joint Circular Memorandum 2013-01.

We recommended the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund to ensure that the unspent balance are readily available for relief and recovery projects and activities within the next five (5) years. Also, we recommend preparing the LDRRMF plan annually and it shall be reviewed and be updated every year and onwards to monitor the activities to be funded by unexpended balances of the LDRRMF.

We recommended requiring the Revenue Collection Officers to strictly turnover all their collections intact and in full amount at the close of the business day to the Municipal Treasurer. Likewise, we recommended that the collections turned over to the Municipal Treasurer including her collections has to be deposited intact to the bank daily or not later than the next banking day. Moreover, we recommended that the Collection Officers and the Municipal Treasurer prepare a monthly certification in their cashbooks of their total monthly cash accountability. Accordingly, we recommended that the accountable officers be properly bonded in accordance with law.
observations.managementAction: Deposit all collections made by the municipality to the depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.
observations.reason: Collections made by agency are not deposited intact. It is the practice of the municipality to accumulate the cash collected before they are deposited to the bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the proposed projects/programs/activities embodied in the approved 20% Development Plan be implemented as planned to improve the socio-economic development of the Municipality and its constituents. Implementation of prioritized projects should be fast tracked to provide stimulus to the Municipality’s progress and development.

We recommended that the municipal's Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement monitoring report duly approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester. Also, make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the procurement processes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive discontinue granting of RATA to the local government employees not entitled to such claim; Allow claim of RATA of designated OIC's but only in reimbursable basis, based on monthly actual performance; Instuct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide for separate appropriation, charge against the appropriate MOOE account for purpose, for the additional allowances of national government officials assigned/ stationed in the municipality; and instruct Municipal Accountant to properly record and account the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that employees will receive no less than the theshold prescribed in the General Appropriations Act in every Fiscal Year, ₱5,000.00 for the ensuing year 2018; evaluate requests for deductions against salaries for the payment of loans and not permit if such will lwer the employee's net take home pay below the threshold, instead, advise employees to personally settle/ directly remit dislodged payments for the loans from private lending intitutions over the counter.
observations.reason: Some of the employees have a net take home pay below the minimum mandatory net pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the person determined liable for audit disallowances to settle/refund the said disallowances immediately in accordance with COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: Out of P95,670.00 disallowance, P89,170.00 were settled as of December 31, 2018. Balance for 2018 were P6,500 only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the local chief executive to: 1. Revisit, review and evaluate the Capital Outlay programs/project/activities included in the Annual Investment Plan of the 20% Economic Development Fund to ensure effective formulation, implementation and utilization of the Fund; 2. Ensure that all projects to be funded from the 20% Development Fund shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure; 3. Adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG- DBM Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2011-1 and 2017-01 dated April 13, 2011 and February 22, 2017, respectively, to ensure that 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcome; 4. Submit the lacking documents and transactions enumerated in Annex B. Ensure 100% complete submission of accounts and transactions by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Some of the 20% EDF still has an MOOE items instead of Capital Outlay. The Municipal Development Coordinator did not regularly monitor thru submission of accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to adhere strictly with the proper accounting and reporting guidelines based on COA Circular 2012- 002 dated September 12, 2012. In particular, we recommend to: a. Transfer to the Special Trust Fund account under Trust Liabilities - DRRMF- MOOE. b. Submit to the COA Auditor's Office the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF so that verification can be made on the said fund. c. Draw Journal Entry to adjust the correct account for the acquisition of rescue equipment. d. Revisit, review and evaluate the programs/project/activities included in the Local Disaster Rist Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plant of the Municipality to ensure effective formulation, implementation and utilization of LDRRM Fund; and e. Ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity-related activities so as to accomplish all planned projects and activities. f. Submit resolutions of the local sanggunian declaring the LGU under the state of calamity or a Presidential of state of calamity upon recommendation of NDRRMC for claims charged against the Quick Response Fund. g. Submit the lacking documents and transactions enumerated in Annex L. Ensure 100% complete submission of accounts and transaction by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Lists of PPAs under the Mitigation Fund and projects those under the Special Trust Fund were prepared yet, but submission of reports were delayed due to minimum manpower assigned in the DRRM Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipality Engineer to see to it that all government PPAs especially infrastructure project are publicized. Adhere strictly to provision set forth under COA Circular No. 2013- 004 dated January 20, 2013

We recommended that the management exert their best effort to fully implement the targeted priority development projects and programs. Strictly administer and supervise the implementation of development projects and likewise monitor its implementation. Determine the reason for non-implementation of some projects and evaluate the possibility to make amendments to some projects which are not feasible for implementation.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to oversee the priority development projects. Also, many funds were downloaded to the LGU from National Government Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management should appropriate 20% Development Fund to develop and operate a landfill to be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.

We recommended The Management should appropriate 20% development fund to capitalized and definite development operation and projects to ensure implementation and completion of projects/programs within the timeframe and to prevent misuse of funds.

We recommended that: The PPAs under the Mitigation Fund and projects those under the Special Trust Fund are provided with details in the LDRRMFIP. Likewise the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF be prepared and submitted to the COA Auditor. Revisit, review and evaluate programs and 100% submission of accounts.
observations.reason: Updates of the records was delayed. Lists of PPAs were prepared but submission of reports was delayed due to minimum manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned official/personnel to: a. Consolidate the PPMPs submitted by the end-user Offices; b. Implement strictly timelines on the posting and submission of procurement reports; c. Establish procurement timelines in consonance with project schedules, including a cut-off date for processing requisitions and issuance of purchase orders to ensure receipt of most of the procured items in time for the project implementation; d. Consider the strict observance of RA 9184 and its IRR, and other existing rule and regulation on government procurement as well as imposition of control measures to ensure that procurement is effected in an efficient, economical and effective manner that is most advantageous to the government and to prevent possible disallowances for such violation.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to focus on the consolidation of PPMPs. There is a need to create internal control to ensure RA 9184 provisions are observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the local chief executive to: immediately restitute the cash shortage of P3,425,442.29 as promised; relieve the Municipal Treasurer to avoid incurrence of additional shortage under her accountability; withheld the salaries and other emoluments due the AO and apply same in payment of indebtedness; and institute appropriate charges against the AO for the missing funds.
observations.reason: Some settlement already made thru salary deduction of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Treasurer and other accountable officers to perform their responsibilities faithfully by depositing immediately and intact all collections with the government depository bank and that the unwarranted failure to fulfill these responsibilities deservse administrative sanction.

We recommended the Management should direct the Accountable Officer to immediately restitute the cash shortage of P602,000.00; stop issuance of additional cash tickets and other accountable forms to the AO and/or relieve him to avoid incurrence of additional cash shortage under his accountability; withheld the salaries and other emoluments due to the AO and apply same in payment of indebtedness; and institute appropriate charges against the AO for the missing funds.
observations.reason: Out of this amount, P452,000 was remitted by the accountable officer and the remaining P150,000.00 was deducted to salary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Government should adopt a system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machines.

We recommended the Municipal Government should undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection efficiency. Moreover, we recommend that Sangguniang Bayan enjoin to impose legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or by judicial auction to enforce the collections of the computed arrearages amounting to P146,575,868.28.
observations.reason: Approval of the auction process and enforcement is still on-going, hence real property records update is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: Require the management to submit the basic financial reports of the LGU within the timeframe as prescribed by the regulations for timely review and audit.
Require the Municipal Office to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers as basis in establishing the RPT Receivable.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to furnish the certified list of taxpayer to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should instruct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to prepare the payrolls while the Municipal Accountant should ensure compliance to the aforementioned provisions of GAA. We further recommended that management discontinue the practice of deducting payments of monthly loan amortizations of private lending establishments from the personnel’s salary and wages payroll. Those personnel whom have loan agreement with private lending institutions must directly remit their payments to the collecting agent/s or office of respective lending establishments.
observations.reason: There are still some employees who have a net take home pay below the minimum requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the BAC to be resolute of its responsibility in ensuring that the LGU abides by the standards set forth in the government procurement law. It may create an internal control to make sure all provisions of RA 9184 are adhered to.

We recommended that the local chief executive enforce immediate refund or return of the balance of unutilized cash advances by withholding the salaries and/or any amount due from accountable officials and employees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw journal vouchers to take up all valid reconciling items in the books of accounts; and b. Make a proper representation with the concerned bank for the adjustment of the LGU bank balances.

We recommended the local chief executive to demand settlement of all cash advances of concerned officials and employees and refrain from approving subsequent cash advances without settlement of previous advances and, also cause for the suspension of those erring accountable officials and employees.

We recommended that Management immediately assess the causes of delays in the project implementation and address all issues accordingly in order to fast track the implementation of priority development projects under the CY 2014 Annual Investment Plan.
observations.reason: Almost all projects has been bidded and others already implemented by the end of December 2015. Municipal Development Coordinator did not regularly monitor thru submission of accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive to secure copies of PNDF Volume 1, current edition, and to direct the Municipal Health Officer and other requisitioning officers to refer as basis for procurements of drugs and medicines accordingly and to supplement certification for each transaction.
observations.reason: Some procurement transactions did not have attached certifications; MHO made reference to safe medicines even without documentation.

observations.recommendation: 1. Direct the concerned officers and employees to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances. 2. Require the Mun. Accountant to effect the necessary correcting entry through the JEV reclassifying the account due from Officers & Employees(123) to Advances to Officers & Employees (148) accounts pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No.2007-001.
observations.reason: Some of the outstanding advances were settled; reclassification entries are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned accountable officers to settle the suspensions immediately and refund the disallowances.
observations.reason: Out of the total, P50,431.67 left for suspensions and P6,500 for notice of disallowance remain unsettled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to: 1. Institute measures to correct the inappropriate practices on semi-expendable supplies, property, plant and equipment; 2. Create a Disposal Committee who shall foresee the inspection, appraisal and disposal of unserviceable properties. Properties deemed not unserviceable must be completed. Application for disposal must be attached with Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property, Individual survey report, certified by the Property Officer or the head of Agency, Stencils of chassis and engine numbers of motor vehicles, Current photographs in two positions and other supporting documents. 3. Instruct the Property Officer to prepare Property Acknowledgement Receipt for all items issued for official use and identification of persons responsible and inventory purposes. 4. Renew PARs every three years after issuance. On returned properties, the original copy of the MR/ARE must be given back to the property officer for cancellation with the duplicate copy. 5. Notify immediately the auditor of the occurrence of the loss. Conduct investigation and prepare report thereon. Within 30 days submit an application for relief supported by the needed documents and affidavits and police report.
observations.reason: Issues on missing items were not yet settled. Some procedures not fully instituted; PARs not completed for all AOs.

observations.recommendation: Present the Logbook of daily attendances as required under Item 4. Section VI of the guidelines, duly signed by each worker; Submit justification on why the cash advance was made by the Disbursing Officer and not by the MSWDO or the In Charge of Office; Submit explanation on why the fund was turned over to the Barangay Chairmen; Submit/attach copy of the Assessment report submitted to the DSWD-FO containing justification/s for extending the number of days for the project to be accomplished; Submit a copy of the Appointment Contracts bearing the signatures of the appointee.
observations.reason: Some required documents were submitted. The same scenario subsisted in the subsequent implementation.

observations.recommendation: Strictly comply with the necessity of procuring supplies, materials and equipment from the PS and equipment from the DBM’s Procurement Service. Communicate with the PSD the status of compliance to the requirement and its inaccessibility. Comply with the above provisions, to be consistent with the national government policy that procurement of government supplies, materials and equipment be done in the most transparent and competitive manner.
observations.reason: There was difficulty in access to PhilGEPS; however communication to PSD and steps to comply were made. Assistance of PhilGEPS in information dissemination was sought.

observations.recommendation: Exert effort to prove the authenticity of the liability. If unable, revert the unestablished liability to Government Equity. Make certain, that the recorded payables are legal, and valid claims against the agency. Verify the account with the NEA.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office had made representation to verify the account with the NEA, however, was not responded to.

observations.recommendation: Strictly comply with the procurement procedures as mandated under the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184) and the Memorandum of Agreement, to deter violation/non-compliance thereof. Also, prepare a supplemental MOA since the contracted amount did not conform to the actual funds utilized.
observations.reason: No update on the compliance; there is a need to prepare supplemental MOA and impose sanctions for non-compliance.

observations.recommendation: Exert effort to prove the authenticity of the liability. If unable, revert the payable to Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 37, Part II, AAR 2011
observations.reason: No relevant document/s was submitted to support the validity of the liability. NEA did not respond.

observations.recommendation: Religiously comply with the requirement so that the IDPs resettlement area will be secured / free from untoward eventualities. The implementing agency and the beneficiaries must accomplish the required documents in order to ascertain its occupancy and the realization of the program.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 40, Part II, AAR 2011
observations.reason: No update from the LGU regarding the compliance of the pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation: Ensure the employees possess competence commensurate with their assigned responsibilities. Provide for an updated and reliable data for complete and accurate information so that errors/misdeeds are readily prevented and detected.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 41, Part II, AAR 2011
observations.reason: Regular and prompt submission of inventory reports was not complied.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated compliance to the audit recommendations to facilitate the count. offered in the previous audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of conducting physical count of LGU properties and submitting updated RPCPPE to the auditor concerned.
observations.reason: No inventory count was made during the year due to lack of personnel designated

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to strictly monitor the effectivity and expiry dates of fidelity bonds of their Accountable Officers and see to it that renewal be made month before their expiry. No Accountable Officers with expired bonds be allowed to continue performing their duties and responsibilities. The Municipal Treasurer should also be instructed to submit to COA every Ist week of January every year, a list of Bonded Officials and Accountable Officers.
observations.reason: The list of all PPAs for CY 2016 was only submitted during the exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better ways of performing its task. The above mentioned guidelines serve as the proper way of structuring the GAD Focal Point System. More projects and activities could be implemented, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, GFPS shall submit the agency’s GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB shall be Jorwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. Actual GAD projects and activities should also be aligned with the GAD plan per COA Circular 14-001 and Joint Circular Memorandum 2013-01.
observations.reason: See related audit observation No. 9

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive Officer, as head of the agency to:
a. Require the General Services Officer to conduct year-end physical inventory on Property,
Plant & Equipment including the CIP-Public Infrastructure accounts and shall submit it to
the Accounting for reconciliation of balances as a requirement to Sec 57 of the NGAS
Manual, Volume I. In the RPCPPE, classify each property, plant and equipment as to
which class/group of assets it belongs.
b. Require the Property and Accounting Unit to maintain complete property and equipment
ledger cards as required under Sec. 43 of NGAS, Volume 1 and Section C. 3, Chapter V
of the Manual on Property Custodianship to ensure correctness and reliability of the PPE
Account balances.
c. Require the Accountant to analyze the discrepancy between the balances in the books
and per physical count of the PPE Accounts.
d. Instruct the Accountant and the General Services Officer to make the necessary
adjustments in their records to take up the noted differences affecting current year’s
transactions.
e. Annually provide a copy of the reconciled Inventory Report to the COA.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirement set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No. 10410
by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for various activities in
support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and comply with the guidelines on
allowable charges against the Special Education Fund set forth in Section 4 of Joint
Circular No. 1. s.2017 of DepED, DBM and DILG; and
b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child
Development Teachers, Day Care Workers and other stakeholders of the ECCD System
in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD programs.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Executive Officer require the General Services Officer
or any authorized official to regularly submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels with
accomplished Drivers’ Trip Ticket for each vehicle/equipment used, including the Monthly
Report of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor for verification, monitoring and
auditing of fuel, oil and lubricants used in accordance with COA Circular 77-61.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management require the Municipal’s Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report duly approved by the head
of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy
Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the
end of each semester. Also, make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and
improving the procurement processes.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to discontinue granting of RATA to the
local government employees not entitled to such claim; Allow claims of RATA of designated
OICs but only in a reimbursable basis, based on monthly actual performance; instruct the
Municipal Budget Officer to provide for separate appropriation, charged against the
appropriate MOOE account for the purpose, for the additional allowances of national
government officials assigned/stationed in the municipality; and instruct Municipal
Accountant to properly record and account the same.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that employees will receive no
less than the threshold prescribed in the General Appropriations Act in every Fiscal Year,
P5,000.00 for the ensuing year 2018; evaluate requests for deductions against salaries for the
payment of loans and not permit if such will lower the employee’s net take home pay below
the threshold, instead, advise employees to personally settle/directly remit dislodged
payments for the loans from private lending institutions over the counter.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the persons determined liable for audit
disallowances to settle/refund the said disallowances immediately in accordance with COA
Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the local chief executive to:
1. Revisit, review and evaluate the programs/projects/activities included in the Annual
Investment Plan of the 20% Economic Development Fund to ensure effective
formulation, implementation and utilization of the Fund;
2. Ensure that all projects to be funded from the 20% Economic Development Fund
shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure;
3. Adhere strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular Nos.
2011-1 and 2017-01 dated April 13, 2011 and February 22, 2017, repectively, to
ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-
economic development and environmental outcomes;
4. Submit the lacking documents and transactions enumerated in Annex B. Ensure
100% complete submission of accounts and transactions by the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to adhere strictly with the proper accounting and
reporting guidelines based on COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12. 2012. In
particular, we recommend to:
a. Transfer to the Special Trust Fund account under Trust Liabilities – DRRM
accounts all the unexpended balance of the 30% Quick Response Fund and the
70% DRRMF – MOOE.
b. Submit to the COA Auditor’s Office the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization
of the LDRRMF so that verification can be made on the said fund.
c. Draw Journal Entry to adjust the correct account for the acquisition of rescue
equipment.
d. Revisit, review and evaluate the programs/projects/activities included in the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan of the Municipality
to ensure effective formulation, implementation and utilization of LDRRM Fund; and
e. Ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity-related activities so as to
accomplish all planned projects and activities.
f. Submit resolutions of the local sanggunian declaring the LGU under the state of
calamity or a Presidential declaration of state of calamity upon recommendation of
the NDRRMC for the claims charged against the Quick Response Fund.
g. Submit the lacking documents and transactions enumerated in Annex L. Ensure 100%
complete submission of accounts and transactions by the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Engineer to see
to it that all government PPAs especially infrastructure projects are publicized. Adhere
strictly to the provision set forth under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management exert their best effort to fully accomplish the
targeted priority development projects and programs embodied in the Annual Investment
Plan pursuant to its mandate. Strictly administer and supervise the implementation of
development projects and likewise monitor its implementation. Determine the reason for
non-implementation of some projects and evaluate the possibility to make amendments to
some projects which are not feasible for implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Honorable Municipal Mayor: (a) direct Officers and Employees with cash advances to immediately submit their liquidation papers, otherwise, require the immediate withholding of salary of those officers and employees who failed to comply with the requirement; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the liquidation of outstanding cash advance and send written demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; (c) stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have ynliquidated cash advances; and (d) take legal actions against previous employees with still unliquidated cash advance who are no longer in the government service.

Also, it is reminded that pursuant to Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 040676 dated June 17, 2004 as amended by CSC Resolution No. 1200103 dated January 12, 2012, the failure of an accountable officer to render an account in full within the periods prescribed and after formal demand shall constitute the administrative offense of Gross Neglect of Duty punishable by dismissal from the service for the first offense. Full liquidation/settiem ent/payment of the subject cash advance outside the given periods shall constitute the offense of Simple Neglect of Duty punishable by suspension from the service for one (1) month and one (1) day to six (6) months for the first offense, and dismissal from the service for the second offense.
observations.managementAction: The Manage- ment committed to comply with the audit recommnen- dation.
observations.reason: Demand letters were already sent to people with accountabiliti es as regards cash advances, however, these people still fail to settle their accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better ways of performing its task. The above mentioned guidelines serve as the proper way of structuring the GAD Focal Point System. More projects and activities could be implemented, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, GFPS shall submit the agency’s GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB shall be forwarded to the DILG Regional Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval. Actual GAD projects and activities should also be aligned with the GAD plar per COA Circular 14-001 and Joint Circular Memorandum 2013-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.

We recommend the Management to appropriate and utilized 20% development fund to concrete and definite development projects/programs and ensure implementation and completion of projects/programs within the timeframe or as calendared

We recommend that Management ensure that the PPAs under the Mitigation Fund and those under the Special Trust Fund are provided with details in the LDRRMFIP.  Likewise the Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF be prepared and submitted to the COA Auditor.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to restitute immediately the cash shortage of P3,425,442.29 as promised; relieve the Municipal Treasurer to avoid incurrence of additional shortage under her accountability; withheld the salaries and other emoluments due the AO and apply same in payment of indebtedness; and institute appropriate charges against the AO for the missing funds.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and other accountable officers to perform their responsibilities faithfully by depositing immediately and intact all collections with the government depository bank and that the unwarranted failure to fulfill these responsibilities deserves administrative sanction.

We recommend that management direct the Accountable Officer to restitute immediately the cash shortage of P602,000.00; stop issuance of additional cash tickets and other accountable forms to the AO and/or relieve him to avoid incurrence of additional cash shortage under his accountability; withheld the salaries and other emoluments due to the AO and apply same in payment of indebtedness; and institute appropriate charges against the AO for the missing funds.

We recommend that the Management direct the Treasury Office to forward immediately the pertinent copies of ORs and validated deposited slips to the Accounting Office which in turn submit them to the Auditing Office.  Also, direct the Accounting Office to demand forwarding of the reports on time with copies of ORs and validated deposit slips and to check the correctness and validity thereof.
We recommend that the Municipality Government adopt a system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machines.

We recommend that Management direct the Accountant to draw JEV right away for the corresponding adjustment entry of the correct amount due from taxpayers for basic and additional levy on real property taxes.  Also, instruct the Accountant further that in the event that e-NGAS updates is not still in place, an adjusting entry at the end of the every year be immediately taken up to bring the amount supplied by the system to a correct figure.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection efficiency. Moreover, we recommend that Sangguniang Bayan enjoin to impose the legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or by judicial auction to enforce the collections of the computed arrearages amounting to P146,575,868.28.

We recommend that the management should implement effective control system on the property management through the following:
The Local Chief Executive shall appoint a General Service Officer that will be responsible in the maintenance and safekeeping of government properties, records, and to ensure the systematic delivery of basic services to the people.
The Local Chief Executive shall require the periodic physical count of property annually and the reconciliation between the accounting and property records after inventory taking.  A report thereat shall be made using the report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be submitted to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the ensuing year; and
The designated GSO shall prepare and issue ARE, instead MR, which shall be renewed every three (3) years; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with the accounting records.
We recommend that the Management ensure all real estate of the LGU are registered under the Torrens Title System and responsible for their proper and effective use and management.

We recommend that the management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct amount balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial position as of a given period of time.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirement for the prompt submission of
the monthly reports together with disbursement vouchers/payrolls/official receipts and
their supporting papers to apprise the Chief Executive about the financial status of the
Mumicipality needed for sound decision making and as means of improving performance
efficiency. Prompt submission of these reports/accounts are required for timely review by
the Auditor and early detection of error or deficiencies.

Fupther, we reminded the Agency to see to it that checks are issued/released to

creditor only if disbursement vouchers are duly processed and signed by the proper
authorities.

We reiterated compliance io the audit recommendations offered in the previous
audit reports which primarily dealt on the assurance of conducting physical count of
LGU properties and submitting updated RPCPPE to the auditor concerned.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office
to strictly monitor the effectivity and expiry dates of fidelity bonds of their Accountable
Officers and see to it that renewal be made month before their expiry. No Accountable
Officers with expired bonds be allowed to continue performing their duties and
responsibilities. The Municipal Treasurer should also be instructed to submit to COA
every Ist week of January every year, a list of Bonded Officials and Accountable
Officers.

We recommended that the agency create a GAD Focal Point System for better
ways of performing its task. The above mentioned guidelines serve as the proper way of
structuring the GAD Focal Point System. More projects and activities could be
implemented, only if the GAD Focal Point System is fully-functional. Also, GFPS shall
submit the agency’s GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and
Development Office (PPDO) within the month of January to February of each year to see
to it that the GPB of the municipality is aligned with the priorities of the provincial
government. Likewise, from the PPDO the GPB shall be forwarded to the DILG Regional
Office within the month of March of each year for appropriate review and approval.
Actual GAD projects and activities should also be aligned with the GAD plar per COA
Circular 14-001 and Joint Circular Memorandum 2013-01.

We recommended the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the
LDRRMEF to the Special Trust Fund to ensure that the unspent balance are readily
available for relief and recovery projects and activities within the next five (5) years.
Also, we recommend preparing the LDRRMF plan annually and it shall be reviewed and
be updated every year and onwards to monitor the activities to be funded by unexpended
balances of the LDRRMF.

The Management should submit the basic financial reports of the LGU within the timeframe as prescribed by the regulations for timely review and audit.  The Management shall adhere strictly at all times to the guidelines provided on the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Institute measures to correct the inappropriate management practices on semi-expendable supplies, property, plant and equipment, viz:



Create a Disposal Committee who shall foresee the inspection, appraisal and disposal of unserviceable properties. Properties deemed unserviceable must be disposed at any manner most appropriate. Application for disposal must be attached with the following:
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property. This form is used for the disposal of vehicles, mechanized equipment, office equipment, furniture and semi-expendable materials. It is accompanied by any of the  following documents, whichever is appropriate-
Individual survey report, duly certified by the Property Officer or the head of Agency
List of missing spare parts duly certified by the Property Officer or Head of Agency.
Stencils of chassis and engine numbers of motor vehicles, and Current photographs in two positions.
Waste Materials Report for Expendable materials, supplies and consumables including spare parts and remnants from destroyed or damaged PPE.
Instruct the Property Officer to prepare Property Acknowledgement Receipt for all items issued for the official use of officials and employees and as bases for identification of persons responsible and inventory purposes. 


Renew PARs every three years after issuance. On returned properties, the original copy of the MR/ARE must be given back to the property officer for cancellation with the duplicate copy.


Notify immediately the auditor of the occurrence of the loss. Conduct investigation and prepare report thereon. Within 30 days submit an application for relief supported by the following documents:

Affidavit executed by the accountable officer stating the following facts:
5.1.1 Property lost and its valuation
5.1.2 Actual date in which the absence was first noted
5.1.3Manner of disappearance
5.1.4 Efforts put forth to recover the same
5.1.5 Provisions made to safeguard the property; and
5.1.6 Date when the loss was reported to th auditor and the police authorities.
5.1.7 Joint Affidavit of two (2) disinterested persons cognizant of the facts and circumstances about the loss. In case it is not possible to obtain the statement of two disinterested persons and only one is available, or none at all, such fact should be  set forth in the affidavit of the person requesting relief, giving the reasons therefore;
  5.1.8 Final Police Report

Done
